text:
Hey everyone! I'm the author of the Lucidity project that was linked to here last week (<http://symbolflux.com/luciditystory.html>) and of a slightly better known structure editor project called Tiled Text (<http://symbolflux.com/projects/tiledtext>).

I've been following Future of Coding stuff from slightly afar via twitter for a year or so, and lurking here for a few days. It looks to me like a great community, and I'm looking forward to talking to more people here, seeing what people are working on, etc.

elements.text: Hey everyone! I'm the author of the Lucidity project that was linked to here last week (
) and of a slightly better known structure editor project called Tiled Text (

).

I've been following Future of Coding stuff from slightly afar via twitter for a year or so, and lurking here for a few days. It looks to me like a great community, and I'm looking forward to talking to more people here, seeing what people are working on, etc.

text: Also, I like the moving between levels for navigation mode (characters, tokens, lines, class members, classes). Do you think another level between lines class members for code blocks would be useful?

elements.text: Also, I like the moving between levels for navigation mode (characters, tokens, lines, class members, classes). Do you think another level between lines class members for code blocks would be useful?

text: Expanding the words to select the characters is also clever. Nice work.

elements.text: Expanding the words to select the characters is also clever. Nice work.

text:
I did a lot of experimentation with that in-between level before arriving at the jump to lines—which I really didn't want to use since I was having to break the more elegant mapping to grammatical units. My memory is that it wasn't often desirable to navigate the detailed hierarchies that would show up there.

Code blocks sounds reasonable though—I certainly had them included at one point :slightly_smiling_face: e.g. dragging a for loop around or whatever.

My ending feeling was still that I hadn't found an optimal breakdown of units that should be made Tiles in the UI. I hadn't seen any other editors like this at the time, so I sort of blindly assumed that directly mapping the "Tiles" to the grammar would be an optimal (or at least non-problematic) UI.

text: Some real gems there, "3 billion devices run Java. That number hasn’t changed in the last 10 years though." ... "A tutorial isn’t really helpful if it’s not a video recording that takes orders of magnitude longer to understand than its text." ... "We don’t use IRC for communication anymore. We prefer a bloated version called Slack because we just didn’t want to type in a server address."

text: GPU programming is extremely difficult and weird. They typically have very limited instructions sets with all sorts of irregularities, and the fact that they often cannot access regular RAM but instead have their own very fast memory makes it the specialty of game programmers and ML number crunchers. Thankfully core counts on regular CPU's are coming up fast due to AMD finally giving Intel some competition again after languishing for so many years. Perhaps some day it will be just one processor type again.

text: Just posted a new example showing the physical units of measurement feature in my Beads language, which tracks units at runtime (by storing the exponents of the component physical unit terms along with the value). A few languages have included physical units, but it is extremely rare to track the units at runtime.

text:
“But it does have to have new ideas about software development. Are you in the right channel?”

Yes. An FoC Demo Day.

“That is what LIVE and the Convivial Salon already do. You can submit a screencast of a demo” 

I’m not sure that’s the same as having a dedicated demo day, or at least not clear to me from looking at the conference site.

text:
Hello everyone !

I'm George ! :wave:
Got invited by <@UDLGND5GA> (thank you very much !) and was lurking for a while :)

I'm a Romanian living in London, nerding away.
I transitioned from graphic design / fine arts fundamentals to programming via Flash / actionscript 2.0 and later 3.0
I got into creative coding toolkits like Processing, MaxMSP, Arduino, old school OpenGL and a touch of OpenFrameworks/OpenCV studying Creative Computing at Goldsmiths, University of London. Later got introduced to a bit of parametric design taking an MSc in Architectural Computation at UCL.
For the past 8 years have been working at Hirsch & Mann (<https://www.hirschandmann.com/work|https://www.hirschandmann.com/work>)
You can see some of the projects I've worked on (mostly Hirsch & Mann TBH) here:
<http://sensori.al|http://sensori.al>

P.S. I recently contributed a Processing library for RunwayML of anyone wants to play: <https://github.com/runwayml/processing-library|https://github.com/runwayml/processing-library>

elements.text:
Hello everyone !

I'm George !
Got invited by

(thank you very much !) and was lurking for a while :)

I'm a Romanian living in London, nerding away.
I transitioned from graphic design / fine arts fundamentals to programming via Flash / actionscript 2.0 and later 3.0
I got into creative coding toolkits like Processing, MaxMSP, Arduino, old school OpenGL and a touch of OpenFrameworks/OpenCV studying Creative Computing at Goldsmiths, University of London. Later got introduced to a bit of parametric design taking an MSc in Architectural Computation at UCL.
For the past 8 years have been working at Hirsch & Mann (
https://www.hirschandmann.com/work

)
You can see some of the projects I've worked on (mostly Hirsch & Mann TBH) here:
http://sensori.al
P.S. I recently contributed a Processing library for RunwayML of anyone wants to play:
https://github.com/runwayml/processing-library

text:
Hi all!

I just wrote a blog-post version of a paper I submitted to PPIG (Psychology of Programming 2019)

The blog post is: <https://blog.coda.io/how-coda-helps-people-overcome-technology-freeze-2132fabd4190>

The actual paper I'll upload here.

I'm sketching out a way to connect recent nervous system breakthroughs to programmable UI design, taking inspiration from how cognitive science breakthroughs anchored original UI design for the Alto.

I'd love to hear what it brings to mind from this community.

text:
Yes lol—it's actually one of my biggest regrets about those projects: I didn't learn to use js/web tech until a couple years after starting Lucidity.

I could've made much faster progress, and it would've been much easier to share :confused:

(As it is, I'd never used a build system at the time, so I don't even have a build script for the projects: I just have to fiddle with IntelliJ for a long time)

elements.text:
